nodes.name: Format Error Entry
nodes.notes: Normalizes whatever failed upstream into one structured shape before it's logged. n8n auto-populates $json.error on error-output items with details about the failure and the node that threw it.

nodes.name: Send Error To Logging Service (Placeholder)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER ENDPOINT — swap for a real error sink before going to production. Good options: Sentry (POST to your DSN's envelope endpoint), Datadog Logs API, or simplest of all, a Slack Incoming Webhook URL posting `{{$json.errorMessage}}` to an #alerts channel. Retry is intentionally NOT configured here — an error-logging call that itself keeps failing shouldn't loop.

nodes.name: Error Logged
nodes.notes: Terminal node — confirms the error branch completed. Nothing downstream depends on this.

parameters.content:
## Shared Error Log

Every HTTP Request node in this workflow has `onError: continueErrorOutput` set, so a failed call doesn't stop the execution — it routes here instead via the node's second (error) output pin. All branches funnel into this one place so there's a single structured error trail rather than scattered failure handling.

Retry: each HTTP Request node retries 3 times with a **fixed** delay (n8n's built-in retry is fixed-delay, not exponential — see each node's notes for how to add real exponential backoff via a Code node if a client needs it).

nodes.name: Lead Intake Webhook
nodes.notes:
Receives the lead payload from src/app/api/leads/route.ts right after its Google Sheets CRM write. responseMode is 'onReceived' so this responds 200 immediately on receipt — the Next.js call is fire-and-forget, it does not wait for the branches below to finish.

TO CONFIGURE: set this node's Authentication to 'Header Auth' and create a credential whose header name is 'x-n8n-secret' and value matches N8N_WEBHOOK_SECRET in the Next.js app's environment — otherwise anyone who finds this URL can post fake leads into the workflow.

After import, copy this node's Production URL into N8N_WEBHOOK_URL in the Next.js app's .env.

nodes.name: Validate & Normalize Payload
nodes.notes: Flattens the webhook's nested `body` wrapper into top-level fields so every downstream node can reference e.g. {{$json.leadId}} directly instead of {{$json.body.leadId}}. This is the only node that needs to change if the Next.js payload shape ever changes.

nodes.name: Is Qualified?
nodes.notes: Routes the qualified branch. False side continues checking maybe_qualified next.

nodes.name: Is Maybe-Qualified?
nodes.notes: Only reached if Is Qualified? was false. False side continues to Is Not-Qualified?.

nodes.name: Is Not-Qualified?
nodes.notes: Only reached if both prior checks were false. False side means an unrecognized status value reached this workflow.

nodes.name: Unexpected Status - Tag For Error Log

string.name: error
string.value: Unrecognized qualification status reached the n8n workflow

nodes.notes: Reached only if qualification.status was something other than qualified / maybe_qualified / not_qualified — which should be impossible given the QUALIFICATION_STATUSES enum in src/types, but this exists so a future change to that enum fails loudly here instead of silently dropping leads.

parameters.content:
## Qualified Branch

Creates the internal task and logs the booking link (already issued by the Next.js route before this workflow even fired). **Does not** wait inline for the booking to happen — see 'Calendly Booking Webhook' trigger lower in this canvas for why, and how the two connect via leadId.

nodes.name: Create Internal Task (Placeholder)

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { title: $json.taskTitle, description: 'Lead: ' + $json.name + ' (' + $json.email + ') — score ' + $json.score + '/10. Next step: ' + $json.recommendedNextStep, dueInDays: 1, leadId: $json.leadId } }}

nodes.notes:
PLACEHOLDER ENDPOINT — replace with the real task tool:
  Asana:    POST https://app.asana.com/api/1.0/tasks (Bearer token auth)
  Trello:   POST https://api.trello.com/1/cards?idList=<LIST_ID> (key+token query params)
  ClickUp:  POST https://api.clickup.com/api/v2/list/<LIST_ID>/task (Bearer token)
Whichever you pick, set its credential on this node and update the URL/body to match that API's task-creation shape.

nodes.name: Log Booking Link Issued
nodes.notes: Structured record that a booking link went out for this lead, viewable in this execution's run data. Not sent anywhere externally by default — wire this node's output to a logging HTTP node (same pattern as the shared error log) if you want it durable outside n8n's own execution history.

nodes.name: Qualified Lead Processed
nodes.notes: Terminal node for this execution. The booking outcome (booked/cancelled) is NOT handled here — it arrives later, in a separate execution, via the 'Calendly Booking Webhook' trigger below, matched back to this lead by leadId. See that trigger's notes for why it has to be a separate trigger rather than a continuation of this one.

parameters.content:
## Maybe-Qualified Branch

Nurture + manual review queue, then a 2-business-day wait before a follow-up reminder fires.

nodes.name: Add To Nurture Sequence (Placeholder)
nodes.notes:
PLACEHOLDER ENDPOINT — replace with the real nurture/ESP tool:
  Mailchimp:      POST https://<dc>.api.mailchimp.com/3.0/lists/<list_id>/members
  ActiveCampaign: POST https://<account>.api-us1.com/api/3/contacts
  Klaviyo:        POST https://a.klaviyo.com/api/profiles
Add this lead to whichever nurture sequence/list is appropriate for a not-yet-clear-cut fit.

nodes.name: Add To Manual Review Queue (Placeholder)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER ENDPOINT — for most clients this ends up being a row in a dedicated 'Review Queue' Airtable base or Notion database rather than a generic API, since a human needs to actually look at it and act. Point this at whichever review surface the founder/team actually checks daily — a queue nobody looks at isn't a queue, it's a leak.

nodes.name: Wait 2 Business Days For Follow-Up
nodes.notes: Pauses this execution for a fixed 48 hours. NOTE: n8n's Wait node counts calendar days, not business days — a lead that lands on a Friday afternoon will get followed up on Sunday, not the following Tuesday. For true business-day-aware scheduling, follow this with an IF node checking {{$now.weekday}} (1-5 = Mon-Fri) and looping back into another short Wait if it lands on a weekend. Left as a fixed 2-day wait here to keep the v1 workflow simple — flagging the gap rather than hiding it.

nodes.name: Send Follow-Up Reminder (Placeholder)

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { leadId: $json.leadId, assignee: 'founder', message: 'Follow up with ' + $json.name + ' at ' + $json.company + ' — maybe-qualified, 2 days have passed.' } }}

nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER ENDPOINT — realistic swaps: a Slack message via a Slack Incoming Webhook (simplest), or a task on the same tool wired up in 'Create Internal Task' above so all follow-ups land in one place.

nodes.name: Maybe-Qualified Lead Processed
nodes.notes: Terminal node for this branch.

parameters.content:
## Not-Qualified Branch

Archive only — the decline email was already sent directly by the Next.js route, so there's nothing further to automate here.

nodes.name: Archive Lead (Placeholder)
nodes.notes: PLACEHOLDER ENDPOINT — in many real deployments this step is actually redundant: the Google Sheets CRM record already has status='not_qualified' from the Next.js write, which IS the archive. Keep this node only if the client wants leads physically moved to a separate sheet/tab, a different CRM pipeline stage, or removed from an active-leads view.

nodes.name: Not-Qualified Lead Archived
nodes.notes: Terminal node for this branch.

parameters.content:
## Why this is a separate trigger

Calendly's webhook subscription needs ONE static URL configured once in Calendly's settings, covering every lead. n8n's Wait node CAN resume on a webhook call, but that resume URL is unique per execution — Calendly has no way to know which per-execution URL to call for which booking. So instead: this is its own always-on trigger that receives every Calendly event for every lead, pulls the leadId back out of Calendly's payload (it was embedded as utm_content when the booking link was built — see src/lib/booking/calendly.ts buildBookingLink()), and reports the outcome back to Next.js. The original lead-intake execution above has already finished by the time this fires, often days later.

nodes.name: Calendly Booking Webhook
nodes.notes:
TO CONFIGURE: in Calendly's webhook subscription settings, point the subscription URL at this node's Production URL, subscribed to the 'invitee.created' and 'invitee.canceled' events. Copy Calendly's signing key into this n8n instance's CALENDLY_WEBHOOK_SIGNING_KEY environment variable (same name as the Next.js app's env var, for consistency — they can hold the same value).

'rawBody: true' is enabled because signature verification needs the exact unparsed request body bytes, not the JSON-parsed version.

nodes.name: Verify Calendly Signature
nodes.notes: Computes and checks the HMAC signature so this endpoint can't be spoofed into reporting fake bookings. See the in-code comment about n8n-version-specific raw-body access if this needs adjustment after import.

nodes.name: Signature Valid?
nodes.notes: Only continues to update Next.js if the HMAC check passed.

nodes.name: Log Invalid Calendly Signature
nodes.notes: Routes signature failures into the shared error log instead of silently dropping them.

nodes.name: Extract Lead ID & Booking Status
nodes.notes: Pulls the lead id back out of Calendly's tracking.utm_content (set when the booking link was built — see buildBookingLink() in src/lib/booking/calendly.ts) and maps Calendly's event name to our bookingStatus vocabulary ('booked' | 'cancelled').

nodes.name: Notify Next.js — Update Booking Status
nodes.notes: Calls back into the Next.js app's POST /api/webhooks/n8n, which patches the CrmRecord's bookingStatus (and flips status to 'booked' when applicable — see that route's handler). TO CONFIGURE: set RELAYOPS_APP_URL (e.g. https://your-app.vercel.app, no trailing slash) as an environment variable on this n8n instance.

nodes.name: Booking Status Synced
nodes.notes: Terminal node — confirms Next.js's CRM record now reflects the real booking outcome.

main.node: Unexpected Status - Tag For Error Log
